title: Enum vs Enum Class in C++
summary: In C++, enumerations (enums) are a powerful tool for creating named sets of integer constants. They help improve code readability and maintainability by providing meaningful names to numeric values. However, C++ offers two ways to define enums: the traditional enum and the more modern enum class. In this blog, we’ll explore the differences between these two…
content: In C++, enumerations (enums) are a powerful tool for creating named sets of integer constants. They help improve code readability and maintainability by providing meaningful names to numeric values. However, C++ offers two ways to define enums: the traditional enum and the more modern enum class. In this blog, we’ll explore the differences between these two options and when to use each with the help of examples.
